title: Change Data Capture: Stop Copying 50M Rows to Move 5K Changes
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-02, Reading time: 7 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpodostack.com%2Fp%2Fchange-data-capture-cdc-intro%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019d4dabe691-8b1c4ed6-5a88-48d1-9d7c-122beaa22cb4-000000/mAoa5hwEqsZP9DTdFpjlDcZko6swYOcBA557DyHcbx4=451
summary: Change Data Capture: Stop Copying 50M Rows to Move 5K Changes (7 minute read) Change Data Capture (CDC) is a technique to efficiently track and stream only the changes from a source database instead of repeatedly copying entire tables. Popular tools include Debezium, Kafka, Fivetran, and Striim. Start simple with timestamps for prototyping and moving to log-based CDC for reliable, low-latency, scalable data synchronization in modern data pipelines.

comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-02, Reading time: 12 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fa10k.co%2Fb%2Freverse-engineering-claude-code-cch.html%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019d4de34a8b-36b4e51d-ce05-464d-b09d-21c5098b058f-000000/-tH7-s20Xt9XWFbBGpjMtek7ntXUUDiLQGNV9o_Vu-w=451
summary: What's cch? Reverse Engineering Claude Code's Request Signing (12 minute read) Anthropic's Claude Code client uses a "cch" hash for request signing, which researchers discovered is an xxHash64 of the request body computed within a custom native Bun runtime. This mechanism serves as a non-cryptographic attribution and metering tool to make sure requests originate from official clients rather than acting as a security boundary.

summary: Granite 4.0 3B Vision: Compact Multimodal Intelligence for Enterprise Documents (6 minute read) IBM's Granite 4.0 3B Vision is a compact multimodal model for enterprise document processing. summary: AWS AI Risk Intelligence (AIRI) for non-deterministic agentic systems (7 minute read) AWS AI Risk Intelligence (AIRI) automates governance for non-deterministic agentic AI systems. This will allow for continuous risk monitoring and actionable recommendations, allowing enterprises to scale autonomous agents while maintaining compliance.

title: Craft is Untouchable
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-02, Reading time: 7 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.chrbutler.com%2Fcraft-is-untouchable%3Futm_source=tldrdesign/1/0100019d4e28b53e-f57097c4-0fee-46a8-ac5d-3927215f429d-000000/j-rKWmfhkz8GbZKx4iftGzgvnoJZMAJrvUOG9AvjsKg=451
summary: Craft is Untouchable (7 minute read) Craft survives AI just as it survived the synthesizer: the tool changes, but the underlying judgment does not. When speed and convenience become the standard, craft doesn't disappear — it just becomes easier to abandon.

summary: Lloyds Data Security Incident Impacts 450,000 Individuals (2 minute read) A faulty software update deployed by Lloyds Banking Group on March 12 exposed mobile banking transaction data to other users for under five hours. Up to 114,182 clicked through to individual transactions, potentially viewing sort codes, account numbers, and National Insurance numbers.

summary: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models (3 minute read) Google DeepMind has released four new vision-capable Apache 2.0-licensed reasoning models sized at 2B, 4B, and 31B, plus a 26B-A4B Mixture-of-Experts. API access to the two larger models is available through Google's AI Studio.

summary: Bad Analogies (18 minute read) Amazon's success has done a great deal of harm to a lot of companies. Jeff Bezos is a generational entrepreneur who came from a hedge fund. Amazon is a bad example of why it is okay to burn loads of cash during growth. The strategy doesn't always work out, especially if it is not executed correctly.

title: A one-line Kubernetes fix that saved 600 hours a year
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-03, Reading time: 10 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fblog.cloudflare.com%2Fone-line-kubernetes-fix-saved-600-hours-a-year%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019d53203efd-8d3764d3-44af-4cce-831c-1dbd3f26b512-000000/UsZbHJOKcPOx0Sl8byyMNPSoZZHkMqrzedKPdUxpdx4=451
summary: A one-line Kubernetes fix that saved 600 hours a year (10 minute read) Cloudflare reduced Atlantis restart times on Kubernetes from 30 minutes to 30 seconds by updating the `securityContext` to `fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch`. This one-line fix prevented Kubernetes from recursively checking millions of file permissions on every restart, reclaiming 600 hours of engineering time annually.

title: Modern SQLite: Features You Didn't Know It Had
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-03, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fslicker.me%2Fsqlite%2Ffeatures.htm%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019d53203efd-8d3764d3-44af-4cce-831c-1dbd3f26b512-000000/gUH6M2lAz9Uou-Td49TVHQItQDadhq4cUGzD8y8DbKg=451
summary: Modern SQLite: Features You Didn't Know It Had (3 minute read) Modern SQLite now extends its capabilities beyond basic storage with features like built-in JSON support and the FTS5 extension for full-text search. For advanced analytics, it has SQL features like window functions and common table expressions, while the write-ahead logging (WAL) mode boosts concurrency and performance.

comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-06, Reading time: 8 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fdropbox.tech%2Finfrastructure%2Fimproving-storage-efficiency-in-magic-pocket-our-immutable-blob-store%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019d6244081e-1a819305-663f-465f-b147-f01a0221af99-000000/P5qFgiaqdqUuBG0kvwKJ1VemuWF3uduhLzctv7sGwMU=451
summary: Improving storage efficiency in Magic Pocket, our immutable blob store (8 minute read) Dropbox improved storage efficiency in Magic Pocket, its custom exabyte-scale immutable blob store, after a new service rollout increased fragmentation and storage overhead, particularly from severely under-filled volumes. summary: NornicDB (GitHub Repo) NornicDB is a low-latency Neo4j-compatible graph + vector database built for AI-native workloads. It combines Cypher queries with GPU-accelerated embedding search and auto-discovery of relationships. NornicDB collapses graph, vector, and memory semantics into one system, enabling evolving knowledge graphs and persistent agent memory without stitching multiple tools together.

title: Data Inlining in DuckLake: Unlocking Streaming for Data Lakes
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-06, Reading time: 13 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fducklake.select%2F2026%2F04%2F02%2Fdata-inlining-in-ducklake%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019d6244081e-1a819305-663f-465f-b147-f01a0221af99-000000/u278jf1Y4P_Z4DzQVz1vBl4Y3tigoeKLimJ7Lqlwqes=451
summary: Data Inlining in DuckLake: Unlocking Streaming for Data Lakes (13 minute read) Data Inlining is a technique that stores small updates (inserts, deletes, or updates below a configurable threshold) directly in its catalog database instead of writing them as tiny Parquet files to object storage. This elegantly solves the classic "small files problem" in data lakes, enabling efficient, low-latency streaming workloads (like sensor data) without constant compaction jobs.

comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-06, Reading time: 5 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpscanf.com%2Fs%2F352%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdevops/1/0100019d627a8e52-9a6ec1c5-7f13-4388-90f6-5eb2c1660494-000000/B83U-hpJ9P35UTuMGGcTkiDxPf2xmO78hJHjzLiJUDQ=451
summary: Can We Measure Software Slop? An Experiment (5 minute read) Software slop is code that hasn't received enough human review or verification. It can be estimated by comparing the attention required (based on code size/complexity) to the attention actually given (via commits, comments, etc.). An experimental “Slop-O-Meter” attempts to quantify this, but results are inconsistent due to differences in development workflows, making the approach promising but not yet reliable.

title: The machines are fine. I'm worried about us
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-06, Reading time: 21 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fergosphere.blog%2Fposts%2Fthe-machines-are-fine%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019d627c9f2e-69f8fd58-b56c-4766-a1c9-7b7acf64c9e3-000000/rIs0KGOkqfi5Rli7-EX7pWlEkibGgZ2k3zrGmpfViNM=451
summary: The machines are fine. I'm worried about us (21 minute read) AI agents threaten the fundamental learning process in academic research, particularly for new scientists. This is illustrated through Alice, who gains deep understanding by tackling problems herself, versus Bob, who uses AI to outsource tasks, producing similar output without developing core skills or intuition. The real danger is a "slow, comfortable drift" where researchers produce results without true comprehension.
